violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered/licensed with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Full time individual certified in food safety is to be registered with the RIDOH CFP as soon as possible. Application supplied to owner via email at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. A chlorine sanitizer bay was set up to properly sanitize equipment at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

At the time of inspection, carnitas, tinga, chicken broth, and corn were observed at 93 to 113 degrees Fahrenheit in then hot hold unit. Food items were properly reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or higher at the time of inspection. (Hot hold unit control knobs observed to be at lowest setting at time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

A wiping rag was observed draped over the edge of the trash bin and then placed onto the cutting board to the prep unit. Employee was instructed by inspector to switch rags and sanitize the cutting board and knife upon notice. Proper sanitizer bucket was made and new rags added at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

The following equipment was observed in need of adjustment or repair: - The gasket to the Single Door Reach-In Cooler in the outdoor storage unit is torn and in need of repair. - The faucet spout to the 3 bay sink in the kitchen is not long enough to reach all 3 bays of the sink to properly fill (per the owner, new faucet is to be acquired for replacement). New Violation. Correct By: 05/25/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of refrigeration units in the outdoor storage unit were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
The mop sink was observed to be lacking a drainpipe. (Per the owner, drainpipe is to be reconnected since prior work done to facility flooring) New Violation. Correct By: 05/25/2026

At the time of inspection, the hot water control to the hand sink was observed to be in poor repair (Water runs with handle turned off). Faucet to the hand sink is to be repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

At the time of inspection, there was no individual certified in food safety, as required, at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to ensure multiple individuals are certified in food safety and to be on site as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered/licensed with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Application for Certified Food Safety Manager License was printed and left with employee at the time of isnpection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

At the time of inspection, a deep pan of Carnitas, prepared previous day, was observed held at 48-49 degrees Fahrenheit in the single door cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. The pan of carnitas was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

A pan of Black Beans were observed held in the Single Door Reach-In at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the beans were left out of refrigeration overnight to defrost. Beans were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
The tile floor in the kitchen area is in poor repair (cracked/chipped exposing subflooring). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

The floor in the refrigeration shed is constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

At the time of inspection, Beans in the Hot Hold Unit were observed held at 125 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Beans were properly reheated to 204 degrees Fahrenheit at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At the time of inspection, large portions of beef and pork were observed cooling in deep covered pans in the kitchen single door reach-in cooler. Methods for proper rapid cooling were discussed. Beef and Pork was transferred to shallow pans and left uncovered for proper rapid cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024

Kitchen knives were observed stored in an unclean surface (tight gap between the prep table and the single door cooler) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Knives were removed for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
The tile floor in the kitchen area is in poor repair (cracked/chipped exposing subflooring). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

The floor in the refrigeration shed is constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

The dumpster outside is located on dirt/gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2023

Beans, which was prepared in advance (night before), was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Beans were placed on the counter to cool overnight. Cooling methods reviewed with PIC to train staff. Cooling logs left on premises to be reviewed by inspector at next visit Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

The dumpster outside is located on dirt/gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

Chicken Tinga and rice, which was prepared in advance (night before), was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Rice and chicken tina was placed into an rice bath and left on the counter overnight. Cooling methods reviewed with PIC to train staff. Cooling logs left on premises to be reviewed by inspector at next visit. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

The dumpster outside is located on dirt/gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen and bathroom was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

Rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Rice was observed at 63-75 degrees Fahrenheit in the single door refrigerator. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Rice was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and covered. Proper cooling methods guide reviewed with PIC and left on site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

The following were observed improperly stored: 1) Knife in-between prep unit 2) Knife stored in plastic wrap container 3) Bowls used as scoops in prep units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

The dumpster outside is located on dirt/gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023
